title: Unable to use the events streaming endpoint as when using docker engine
body:
### Issue Description

I am unable to consume events using the `/events` endpoint.

Sample code to reproduce it

Sample `package.json` - note, using axios 1.7.7 as it supports streaming out of the box using http agent.

```json
{
  "name": "podman-events-streaming-test",
  "description": "",
  "main": "test.mjs",
  "homepage": "./",
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "node": "node"
  },
  "private": true,
  "dependencies": {
    "axios": "^1.7.7"
  }
}
```

The example `test.mjs`

```javascript
import axios from "axios";
import httpAdapter from "axios/lib/adapters/http.js";
import http from "http";

export function createApiDriver() {
  const httpAgent = new http.Agent({
    keepAlive: true,
    keepAliveMsecs: 10,
    timeout: 30000
  });
  const driver = axios.create({
    adapter: httpAdapter,
    httpAgent,
    baseURL: "http://d",
    // socketPath: "\\\\.\\pipe\\podman-machine-default"
    socketPath: "/run/user/1002/podman/podman.sock"
    // socketPath: "/var/run/docker.sock"
  });
  return driver;
}

const driver = createApiDriver();
console.debug(">> PING");
let response = await driver.get("/_ping");
console.debug("<< PING", response.data);
console.debug(">> EVENTS STREAM");
response = await driver.get("/events", { responseType: "stream" });
// response.data.pipe(process.stdout);
console.debug("<< EVENTS STREAM (waiting for events)");
```

To test

```
npm install
node test.mjs
```


### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue

1. Use example client code to reproduce the issue


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received

Upon running the example, although the response type is `stream`, the request never finishes

```
>> PING
<< PING OK
>> EVENTS STREAM
```

Interception of events is not possible because the request never finishes.

### Describe the results you expected

Describe the results you expected

When switching to docker socket (docker engine), the output looks like this, which is expected:

```
>> PING
<< PING OK
>> EVENTS STREAM
<< EVENTS STREAM (waiting for events)
```

And one can now intercept events and use them

### podman info output

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

I've tried podman on linux natively, on macos and lima, all behave the same.

### Additional information

The issue happens in any os

comments.body: This sounds like a duplicate of https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/23712
Closing this as dup, PR https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/24406 contains the fix so feel free to test it.
